text:
0:33 "400 minutes a day, 300× a week"

7 days[1] × 400 minutes each = 2800 minutes spent on the HoH SiS a week

400 minutes going into 60[1] = 6 hours 40 minutes, or 6 2/3 hours, daily

300 times weekly ÷ 7 days = 42.8571428571 times daily

42.8571428571 daily ÷ 24 hours = 1.78571428571 times hourly 
(× 60 minutes[1], that's 107.142857143 minutes each)

1.78571428571 hourly × 6 hours = 10.7142857143
(1.78571428571 hourly ÷ 3 thirds of a seventh hour spent) × 2 from its numerator = 1.19047619047 extra

10.7142857143 + 1.19047619047 = 11.9047619048 times per daily session

So according to my calculations– if my math is correct– not only would you have to spend more than 27.777777777778%[2] on the Blowflex to get the JoJ done as-advertised, but in that daily time frame, you'd have to do it all over again ~12 times each, nearly twice per hour. And that's assuming it is done at a constant rate without breaks. Albeit, a single Blowflex sexercise taking about an hour or so seems about right by itself.

In conclusion, much like many other exercise/fitness products advertised on TV and such, its inflating its numbers to seem like a better deal. For a YTP, doing it unintentionally, that's pretty accurate.

[1] With whole number time conversions used; 60 minutes per hour, 24 hours per day, 7 days per week.

[2] 2800 ÷ 10080 = 0.27777777777778 = 27.777777777778%: in other words, it is 27.777777777778% of the entire week, nonstop.
author: @BinglesP

text:
Bonus calculation:

Rounding up a year to take 52 weeks, results in 90 weeks would take 1 year, with an additional 38 for the second year left over.

Using [2] as a shortcut, 27.777777777778% of 365 = 101.3888888888 days' worth of constant sexercise.

In order to actually "get results" on this thing, you'd have to do it 27,000 times in the recommended weekly increments scheduled. And, even if you could spend 100% of each day constantly doing this, you'd still have to spend more than 100 days total on it alone.

100% unsatisfied.
